title: A2UI Atoms Catalog
description: The A2UI multi-surface atom vocabulary as an A2UI-v1.0 catalog document. Each stable atom is a component; a host resolves createSurface.catalogId (this file's URL) to it.
instructions: Every key under `components` is a valid `component` value (469 components). Compose layouts with Column/Row containers; each component's fields are its properties. Canonical URI: https://a2uicatalog.ai/catalogue/a2ui-atoms-v1.json — full prose spec (descriptions, surfaces, attribution): https://a2uicatalog.ai/spec.json

gauge_sla.description: Renders a semi-circular radial gauge track with high contrast status quadrants, a glowing needle tick, and a prominent central KPI readout.

stacked_area.description: Renders cumulative layered area chart trends utilizing overlapping translucent gradient fills and glowing series borders.

labels.description: list of strings representing X-axis ticks (e.g., ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4'])

series.description: list of dictionaries representing stacked layers: [{'label': 'Enterprise', 'data': [10, 25, 45, 60], 'color': '#00f2ff'}, ...]

scatter_trend.description: Renders a coordinate scatter grid representing discrete data points intersected by a prominent glowing linear regression line.

title.description: string (optional, e.g., 'CSAT vs. Support Response Time')

call_mood_board.description: Renders a premium, aesthetic call emotion summary and theme board showing detected sentiments, dominant moods with color codings, active themes, and word weightings.

moods.description: list of dictionaries representing call emotions: [{'mood': 'Collaborative', 'intensity': 85, 'color': '#10b981'}, ...]

github_activity_grid.description: Renders a high-fidelity SVG representation of a GitHub-style contribution activity calendar with colored commitment density blocks, total contributions, and streak statistics.

activity.description: list of integers (0-4) or dictionary of weeks/days for rendering grid squares

form.description: Form container with labelled field controls and explicit submit/cancel buttons. Fields are expressed as a list of {label, type, name, placeholder, options, rules} entries.

title.description: string (optional). Form heading shown above fields.

submit_label.description: string (optional, default 'Submit'). Label for the primary submit button.

cancel_label.description: string (optional). If set, renders a secondary cancel button.

fields.description: {'type': 'array', 'items': {'label': 'string. Visible field label.', 'name': 'string. Field identifier used in form submission.', 'type': 'string. One of: text, email, password, number, url, textarea, select, radio, checkbox, switch, slider, date. Default: text.', 'placeholder': 'string (optional).', 'options': 'array of {value, label} (required for select, radio, checkbox types).', 'default_value': 'string | boolean | number (optional).', 'rules': "array of strings (optional). Validation rules e.g. ['required', 'email', 'minLength:2']."}}

form_input.description: Standalone labelled text input field — text, email, password, number, or url. Use inside a form atom or as a standalone search/filter control.

label.description: string. Visible label above the input.

type.description: string (optional). One of: text, email, password, number, url. Default: text.

form_select.description: Labelled dropdown select with a list of value/label options. Supports required validation and an optional placeholder prompt.

form_radio_group.description: Labelled group of radio buttons for single-option selection. Each option has a value, visible label, and optional description.

form_checkbox_group.description: Labelled group of checkboxes for multi-option selection. Each checkbox has a name, visible label, optional description, and optional default checked state.

name.description: string. Aggregate field identifier for form submission.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {name, label, description?, default_checked?} entries.'}

form_switch_group.description: Group of named toggle switches — on/off controls with optional labels and descriptions. Suitable for settings panels, notification preferences, and feature flags.

label.description: string (optional). Section label above the switches.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {name, label?, description?, default_checked?} entries.'}

form_slider.description: Numeric range slider with a min, max, and optional step. Supports continuous (smooth) and discrete (stepped) variants. Renders a labelled track with a draggable thumb.

label.description: string (optional). Visible label above the slider.

step.description: number (optional). Increment size. Omit for continuous.

variant.description: string (optional). One of: continuous, discrete. Default: continuous.

form_date_picker.description: Date picker input supporting single-date selection or a date-range (start + end). Renders a calendar popover. Use inside a form or standalone.

mode.description: string (optional). One of: single, range. Default: single.

modal.description: Modal dialog overlay with a title, configurable size, and arbitrary content children. Opened programmatically or via a trigger button. Closes on X, Escape, or backdrop click.

size.description: string (optional). One of: sm, md, lg. Default: md.

children.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'Content atoms to render inside the modal body.'}

trigger_label.description: string (optional). If set, renders a button that opens the modal.

follow_up_chips.description: A row of tappable suggestion chips placed at the end of a response or slide, prompting the user with pre-written follow-up questions or actions. Each chip fires its text as a new user message when clicked.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': "List of suggestion strings. e.g. ['What's the ROI?', 'Show me by region', 'Compare to last year']."}

label.description: string (optional). Small heading above the chips. e.g. 'You might also ask:'

choicebox_group.description: Card-style option selector where each option renders as a full card with icon, title, and description. The entire card is the click target — richer than radio buttons. Inspired by Vercel Geist Choicebox. Ideal for plan selection, framework choice, and AI-generated setup wizards.

label.description: string (optional). Group heading above the cards.

name.description: string. Form field name for the selected value(s).

multiple.description: boolean (optional, default false). If true allows multiple selections.

submit_label.description: string (optional). If set, renders a submit button below the cards.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {value, title, description?, icon?, disabled?} entries.'}

feedback_prompt.description: In-context feedback collection widget for rating content or AI response quality. Supports thumbs up/down or 1–5 star ratings with optional follow-up text. Inspired by Vercel Geist Feedback pattern.

question.description: string (optional). Label text above the widget. e.g. "Was this helpful?"

style.description: string (optional). One of: thumbs | stars. Default: thumbs.

placeholder.description: string (optional). Follow-up textarea placeholder shown after the rating buttons.

action_url.description: string (optional). Endpoint for form POST on submit.

entity_list.description: List of named resources — each row shows an avatar or icon, a title, a subtitle, a status badge, and optional trailing metadata. Covers project lists, deployment rosters, and team member panels. Inspired by Vercel Geist Entity component.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {name, subtitle?, icon?, status?, meta?} entries.'}

prompt_template.description: Displays an LLM prompt with interpolation slots highlighted in a distinct colour. Includes an optional copy-to-clipboard button. Ideal for AI documentation, tutorials, and prompt engineering articles.

template.description: string. The prompt text containing {slot} placeholders.

copyable.description: boolean (optional, default true). Show copy button.

label.description: string (optional). Small label shown above the template block.

model_card.description: AI model specification card showing model name, provider, context window, pricing tier, and a row of capability badges. First-class atom for AI-native content and agent documentation.

name.description: string. Model display name, e.g. "Claude Sonnet 4.6".

capabilities.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of short capability badge strings, e.g. ["tool use", "vision", "streaming"].'}

accent.description: string (optional, default #7c3aed). Accent colour for provider label and badges.

conversation_snippet.description: Renders a prompt-response pair as two chat bubbles — a user bubble on the right and an AI response bubble on the left. Ideal for showing LLM examples, chatbot flows, and agent interaction patterns in documentation.

user_label.description: string (optional, default "You"). Label above the user bubble.

ai_label.description: string (optional, default "Assistant"). Label above the AI bubble.

shortcut_legend.description: Keyboard shortcut cheat-sheet grid. Multiple shortcuts displayed in a compact two-column layout, each showing the key combination and its action label. Distinct from keyboard_shortcut which shows a single combination inline.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {keys, action} entries. keys is an array of key strings e.g. ["⌘", "K"]. action is the human-readable description.'}

rating_summary_bar.description: Aggregate star-rating histogram showing the percentage breakdown per star level (5★ through 1★). Distinct from rating_stars which shows a single score — this shows the full distribution with bar lengths proportional to vote share.

roadmap_card.description: Quarter-based product roadmap showing milestones across Q1–Q4 or custom periods. Each period contains a list of items with a status indicator (done, in-progress, planned). Useful for product announcements and release planning slides.

periods.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {label, items} where each item is {text, status}. status is one of done | in-progress | planned.'}

notification_stack.description: Persistent notification inbox showing a list of notification items, each with an icon or emoji, a title, a body snippet, a timestamp, and an optional unread indicator dot. Distinct from toast_notification which is a transient single pop-up — the stack is a persistent grouped list.

title.description: string (optional). Heading above the list, e.g. "Notifications".

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {icon?, title, body?, time?, unread?} entries. unread is boolean, default false.'}

inline_alert.description: Compact inline-level alert embedded within content flow — an icon and short message that appears beside labels, form fields, or within paragraphs without disrupting layout. Distinct from alert_banner (full-width strip) and toast_notification (transient overlay).

detail.description: string (optional). A secondary line of smaller detail text.

icon.description: string (optional). Override the default icon for the severity type.

tag_block.description: Horizontal wrapping block of plain content tags — feature labels, category chips, or keyword pills. Neutral styling. Distinct from badge_group which carries semantic status colour. Adapted from the TagBlock pattern in OpenUI OUI benchmark samples.

tags.description: array of strings. The tag labels.

color.description: string (optional). Override chip accent — hex or CSS colour. Default is neutral gray.

variant_selector.description: CSS-only variant picker — each option is a selectable card with a label and optional description, rendered as hidden radio inputs with label:has(input:checked) styling. Used for product size, colour, or plan variant selection. Adapted from the RadioGroup/RadioItem pattern in OpenUI OUI benchmark samples.

name.description: string. Form field name used in radio input group.

label.description: string (optional). Group label shown above the options.

items.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {value, title, description?} option objects.'}

markdown_block.description: Renders an arbitrary GitHub-Flavoured Markdown string as formatted HTML — bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, lists, tables, blockquotes, and links. Useful when content is authored in or retrieved as Markdown and needs faithful rendering without mapping to individual atoms. Adapted from the MarkDownRenderer pattern in OpenUI OUI benchmark samples.

content.description: string. The Markdown source string to render.

variant.description: string (optional). "default" (standard margins) or "compact" (tight spacing for dense layouts). Default is "default".

chartjs_pie.description: Multi-slice pie or donut chart rendered as inline SVG — category proportions with a colour-coded legend. Distinct from donut_stat which shows a single metric as a CSS ring. Adapted from the PieChart/Slice pattern in OpenUI OUI benchmark samples.

colors.description: array of strings (optional). Hex colours per slice; auto-assigned if omitted.

show_legend.description: boolean (optional). Show colour-keyed legend below chart. Default true.

show_labels.description: boolean (optional). Show percentage labels on slices. Default true.

text_callout.description: Compact tinted note or tip block used inline within cards, forms, or panels. Lighter than callout — no left-border accent, no icon; just a soft colour-tinted background with a small bold title above body text. Adapted from the TextCallout pattern in OpenUI OUI benchmark samples.

title.description: string. Short bold label (e.g. "Tip", "Note", "Good to know").

source_citation.description: Inline-numbered RAG source reference card — shows a citation number, source title, optional excerpt, and optional URL. Designed to accompany AI-generated content that cites retrieved documents. Pairs with in-body superscript references [1], [2] etc. Original a2ui-catalogue atom.

sources.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {number, title, url?, excerpt?, author?, date?} citation objects.'}

heading.description: string (optional). Section heading above the list, e.g. "Sources".

llm_comparison_table.description: Side-by-side comparison of outputs from multiple language models for the same prompt — model name, output text, and optional latency/cost/token metadata per column. Designed for model evaluation, A/B testing, and capability demonstrations. Original a2ui-catalogue atom.

prompt.description: string (optional). The shared input prompt shown above the comparison.

models.description: {'type': 'array', 'description': 'List of {name, output, latency_ms?, cost_usd?, tokens?} model result objects.'}

show_meta.description: boolean (optional). Show latency/cost/token row below each output. Default true if any model provides meta fields.

confidence_bar.description: Horizontal confidence or probability bar — shows a percentage value with a label and colour-coded fill indicating certainty level. Used for classification confidence, sentiment strength, retrieval relevance scores, or any normalised probability output. Original a2ui-catalogue atom.

label.description: string. What is being measured, e.g. Positive Sentiment or Retrieval Relevance.

items.description: array (optional). List of {label, value} for multi-row display instead of single bar.

color.description: string (optional). Override bar fill colour. Auto-assigned green/amber/red by value band if omitted.

token_budget_meter.description: Visual context-window usage meter — shows tokens consumed versus the model's total context limit, with a colour-coded fill that shifts amber then red as capacity is approached. Designed for AI agent dashboards, session monitoring, and prompt-engineering tooling. Original a2ui-catalogue atom.

total.description: integer. Model context window size, e.g. 200000.

model.description: string (optional). Model name shown as subtitle, e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-6".

label.description: string (optional). Override the default "Context window" heading.

warn_at.description: number (optional). Percentage threshold to shift to amber. Default 70.

critical_at.description: number (optional). Percentage threshold to shift to red. Default 90.

animate.description: boolean (optional). Count up from 0 to `used` using CSS @property animation. Bar grows in sync. Uses dark styling suited to Meet stage. Default false (static).

duration.description: number (optional). Animation duration in seconds when animate is true. Default 2.0.

product_thumbnail.description: Shopify Polaris-style product card showing optional image, title, vendor, SKU, price with optional compare-at strike-through, status badge, and tag chips. Adapted from Polaris ResourceItem + Thumbnail patterns.

status.description: string (optional). One of active | draft | archived. Default active.

tags.description: array of strings (optional). Flat tag list rendered as chips.

order_status_card.description: Shopify Polaris-style order summary card showing order number, date, fulfilment status badge, customer name, line item list, and total. Status is colour-coded — fulfilled (green), unfulfilled (amber), partial (blue), cancelled (red), refunded (grey). Adapted from Polaris Card + Badge patterns.

date.description: string (optional). Order date displayed next to the order number.

status.description: string. One of fulfilled | unfulfilled | partial | cancelled | refunded.

items.description: array (optional). List of {title, qty, price} line items.

inventory_table.description: Shopify Polaris-style inventory management table showing SKU, product name, available quantity, committed quantity, and warehouse location per row. Rows with available stock below the optional threshold are highlighted amber as a low-stock warning. Adapted from Polaris DataTable patterns.

items.description: array. List of {sku?, product, available, committed?, location?, threshold?}. threshold triggers amber low-stock highlight when available < threshold.

jira_ticket.description: Atlassian Design System Jira issue card showing issue key, type icon (bug/story/task/epic/subtask), one-line summary, status lozenge, priority indicator, assignee, optional description, and label chips. Adapted from Atlassian Design System Card + Lozenge patterns.

issue_type.description: string (optional). One of bug | story | task | epic | subtask. Default task.

summary.description: string. Issue title or one-line description.

status.description: string (optional). Column name e.g. "To Do", "In Progress", "Done".

priority.description: string (optional). One of highest | high | medium | low | lowest.

description.description: string (optional). Short description or acceptance criteria.

labels.description: array of strings (optional). Label chips shown below the description.

sprint_board.description: Atlassian Design System Jira-style sprint board rendered as kanban columns. Each column has a name, ticket-count badge, and compact issue cards (key, summary, type icon, priority dot). Distinct from prerequisite_checklist which is a linear checklist. Adapted from Atlassian Design System Board patterns.

sprint_name.description: string (optional). Sprint label shown above the board.

columns.description: array. List of {name, items[]} where each item is {key, summary, type?, priority?}.

lozenge.description: Atlassian Design System semantic status pill with six strictly defined appearance variants — default (grey), success (green), removed (red), inprogress (blue), moved (yellow), new (teal). Uppercase rounded-rectangle label. Distinct from badge_group which uses arbitrary colours; lozenge maps to Jira/Confluence status semantics. Adapted from Atlassian Design System Lozenge component.

text.description: string. The lozenge label (single lozenge mode).

appearance.description: string (optional). One of default | success | removed | inprogress | moved | new. Default default.

items.description: array (optional). List of {text, appearance} for a row of multiple lozenges. Overrides single text/appearance fields.

data_grid.description: IBM Carbon Design System enterprise data grid with typed columns (string/number/status/tag), optional row-selection checkboxes, sortable column indicators, zebra-stripe rows, and optional pagination footer. More capable than table and data_table_sortable for enterprise data-dense layouts. Adapted from IBM Carbon DataTable patterns.

title.description: string (optional). Dark header bar above the grid.

columns.description: array. List of {header, key, type?, sortable?}. type is one of string | number | status | tag.

rows.description: array. List of row objects where each key matches a column key.

selectable.description: boolean (optional). Show row-selection checkboxes. Default false.

pagination.description: object (optional). '{per_page} splits rows into CSS-tab pages; clicking a page number label switches pages without JavaScript. Omit for single-page display.'

tree_view.description: IBM Carbon Design System hierarchical tree for displaying recursive data structures — organisational charts, category hierarchies, nested permission models, BOM trees. More semantically general than file_tree which is file-system specific. Expanded/collapsed state set per node. Adapted from IBM Carbon TreeView component.

title.description: string (optional). Label above the tree panel.

nodes.description: array. Recursive list of {label, icon?, expanded?, meta?, children?[]} node objects.

heatmap_calendar.description: IBM Carbon-inspired calendar heatmap showing activity density by date across one or more calendar months. Data is a list of date-value pairs; cells are coloured by intensity across a configurable colour scale. Distinct from heatmap (arbitrary x/y grid) and github_activity_grid (year-spanning GitHub-specific format). Adapted from IBM Carbon AI Applications data visualisation patterns.

title.description: string (optional). Heading above the calendar grid.

data.description: array. List of {date, count} objects where date is "YYYY-MM-DD".

months.description: integer (optional). Number of calendar months to render. Default 3.

color_scale.description: array of strings (optional). CSS colours from empty to maximum density. Default IBM Carbon blue scale.

unit.description: string (optional). Unit label appended to count in tooltips, e.g. "commits".

combobox.description: Searchable filterable dropdown that narrows its option list as the user types. Renders with a search icon, chevron, and an open option list for article preview. Native <datalist> enables browser-level autocomplete.

label.description: string (optional). Visible label above the field.

feature_grid.description: A responsive CSS grid of feature tiles, each with an icon, title, and description paragraph. Supports 2 or 3 columns and optional per-tile badges. The canonical Tailwind UI marketing feature section pattern.

heading.description: string (optional). Section heading above the grid.

description.description: string (optional). Section sub-heading or intro paragraph.

features.description: array of objects with fields — icon (emoji or text), title, description, badge (optional short label), color (optional hex accent).

navigation_menu.description: Multi-level horizontal navigation bar with a brand/logo slot, top-level nav items, optional dropdown submenus, and a right-aligned CTA button. First item with children renders its submenu panel open for preview. Radix NavigationMenu pattern.

brand.description: string (optional). Brand name shown on the left.

brand_url.description: string (optional). URL for the brand link.

items.description: array of objects with — label, url, children (optional array of {label, url, description}).

cta.description: object (optional). {label, url} for a right-aligned call-to-action button.

multi_select_input.description: Tag/chip style multi-value selector. Displays selected values as removable chips above a filtered option list. Shadcn multi-select pattern.

label.description: string (optional). Visible label above the field.

placeholder.description: string (optional). Input placeholder when no chips are selected.

selected.description: array of value strings. Pre-selected values shown as chips.

otp_input.description: One-time password entry field. Renders N individual digit boxes (default 6) with filled/unfilled visual state. Shadcn InputOTP pattern.

length.description: integer (optional). Number of digit boxes. Default 6.

label.description: string (optional). Visible label above the boxes.

skill_bars.description: List of labeled horizontal progress bars — each with a fill percentage, optional sublabel, and per-bar color. Use for competency profiles, CV pages, feature comparison.

accent.description: string (optional, default hex for all bars)

height.description: integer (optional, bar height in px, default 10)

skills.description: array (required, alias items). Each: {label, value (0–100, alias percent), color?, sublabel?}

icon_stat_row.description: Horizontal strip of stats each with a large emoji icon above the number and a label below. More visual than metric_row — use when icons reinforce meaning.

stats.description: array (required, alias items). Each: {icon (emoji), value, label, prefix?, suffix?, sub?, accent?}

color_section.description: Renders child atom blocks inside a colored background container. Use to visually group content with tint, solid, or dark style.

tag_cloud.description: Visual cloud of tags with variable font size based on weight. Heavier tags appear larger. Use for skill tags, topic clouds, keyword displays.

min_size.description: integer (optional, minimum font size px, default 12)

max_size.description: integer (optional, maximum font size px, default 22)

tags.description: array (required, alias items). Each: {label, weight? (default 1), color?} or plain string

step_progress.description: Horizontal step progress indicator with numbered circles and connecting lines. Completed steps show a tick. Use at the top of wizard or onboarding flows.

split_pane.description: Two-panel split layout with distinct background colors per pane. Each pane renders atom blocks independently. Unlike columns, each side has its own background.

gradient_hero.description: Full-width light/pastel gradient hero with badge, large title, subtitle, and optional CTA. Pure CSS — no images. Works on all surfaces. Use when dark_hero is too heavy.

icon_list.description: List of items each with a colored icon circle, label, and text. More visual than bullet_list. Use for feature lists, how-it-works, benefits.

accent.description: string (optional, default hex for all items)

items.description: array (required). Array of {icon (emoji), label (optional), text (optional), color (optional hex)}

highlight_box.description: A visually rich highlighted box with gradient, solid, or outline style. Richer than callout — no severity system, pure design focus.

text.description: string (optional, markdown — renderer degrades to empty; provide title and/or text)

two_tone_card.description: Card with a solid colored header section (title, subtitle, icon) and a white body that renders atom blocks. Clean, elegant container for any content.

blocks.description: array (optional, atom blocks rendered in white body)

metric_row.description: Horizontal strip of static metrics — value, label, optional prefix/suffix and trend indicator. Cleaner than animated_counter for non-animated use.

accent.description: string (optional, default hex for all metrics)

metrics.description: array (required, alias items). Each item: {value, label, prefix?, suffix?, sub?, accent?, trend? ("up"|"down")}

numbered_list.description: Numbered list with either large decorative number backgrounds ("large" style) or circular number badges ("badge" style). More visual than steps.

items.description: array (required). Array of {label (optional), text (optional)}

page_header.description: Full-width app-style page header with title, subtitle, icon, accent color, and optional tag badge. Sets the visual tone of a pop-up app or brief. Use as the first block of any page.

tag.description: string (optional, badge label shown in accent pill)

back_button.description: Styled back navigation button. Links to a URL, a named page slug, or browser history. Three visual styles.

section_break.description: Labeled or plain section divider. Supports solid, dashed, or dotted line styles. With a label, renders text centered in the line (Notion-style).

chip_group.description: Row of filter/tag chips, optionally scrollable. Chips can link to URLs and have active state.

chips.description: array (required). Array of {label, color? (hex), url? (href), active? (bool)}

columns.description: Generic multi-column layout container. Each column holds an array of atom blocks rendered inline. 2–6 columns; collapses to single column on mobile.

items.description: array (required). Each item is {blocks: [atom blocks]} — one entry per column.

person_card.description: Individual person card with name, role, optional photo, bio, tags, and contact links. Use inside a columns atom to build team rosters.

agenda_block.description: Time-slotted schedule view for a day or event. Each slot has a time, title, optional speaker, location, type badge, and description.

slots.description: array (required). Array of {time, title, speaker?, location?, type? ("break"|"keynote"|"workshop"|"panel"|"social"), description?}

risk_flag.description: Structured risk callout list with severity levels, description, and mitigation. For meeting prep, project status, incident reports.

risks.description: array (required). Array of {level ("critical"|"high"|"medium"|"low"), title, description?, mitigation?}

action_items.description: Action items table with owner, due date, and status. For retros, meeting notes, sprint planning, incident reports.

items.description: array (required). Array of {action, owner?, due?, status? ("open"|"in_progress"|"done")}

bento_grid.description: Asymmetric CSS grid of feature tiles. First tile typically spans multiple columns for visual emphasis. Supports per-tile icons, titles, descriptions, and accent colors. MagicUI/shadcn bento pattern.

heading.description: string (optional). Section heading above the grid.

columns.description: integer (optional). Number of grid columns. Default 3.

tiles.description: array of objects with — title, subtitle, icon (emoji), span (integer, default 1; span 2 fills two columns), color (optional hex), background (optional hex).

cta_section.description: Full-width call-to-action banner with headline, body text, a primary button, and an optional secondary ghost button. Renders on a solid color background. Tailwind UI CTA section pattern.

primary_cta.description: object. {label, url} for the primary action button.

secondary_cta.description: object (optional). {label, url} for a ghost secondary button.

background.description: string (optional). CSS color for the banner background. Default

animated_counter.description: CSS @property counter animation that counts up from zero to target values without JavaScript. Uses @keyframes with CSS custom properties typed as integers. Each counter has a value, label, optional prefix/suffix, and color.

counters.description: array of objects with — value (integer), label, prefix (optional), suffix (optional), color (optional hex).

duration.description: number (optional). Animation duration in seconds. Default 2.

media_stream_card.description: Auto-detecting media embed that accepts a raw URL and builds the correct iframe for YouTube, Loom, Google Slides, or Vimeo. Displays a labelled card shell with a skeleton shimmer while loading. Designed for stage-first delivery where the URL is the only payload.

url.description: string (required). Raw URL — YouTube, Loom, Google Slides, Vimeo, or any embeddable URL. Platform is auto-detected.

title.description: string (optional). Card label shown above the iframe. Defaults to detected platform name.

height.description: string (optional). CSS height for the iframe container. Default 360px.

live_aggregator.description: Comparative progress-bar display for real-time vote/response data. Accepts an items array of label+value pairs and renders normalised horizontal bars with optional value labels. Designed for stage-first delivery where items update incrementally via fiber.

items.description: array of objects — label (string), value (number), color (optional hex).

title.description: string (optional). Section heading above the bars.

max_value.description: number (optional). Denominator for bar widths; auto-computed from max item value if omitted.

show_values.description: boolean (optional). Show numeric value next to each bar. Default true.

vote_button_group.description: Styled multi-option vote selector with three visual variants — pill (rounded filled), neon (dark with cyan glow), and default (flat bordered). Encodes the Cyberpunk Maverick voting pattern as a schema atom. CSS-only interactivity using checkbox/radio inputs.

options.description: array of objects — label (string), value (string), selected (boolean optional).

style.description: string (optional). One of pill, neon, default. Default pill.

title.description: string (optional). Section heading above the buttons.

allow_multi.description: boolean (optional). Allow multiple selections. Default false.

effect_overlay.description: CSS-animated celebration overlay triggered by a named effect type. confetti fires falling coloured rectangles via keyframe animation. trophy bounces a large emoji with a scale-in animation. pulse radiates a glowing circle. fireworks scatters particles radially. Designed as a schema atom wrapper around the canvas particle blast used in Cyberpunk Maverick demos.

trigger.description: string (required). One of confetti, trophy, pulse, fireworks.

status.description: string (optional). Small label shown above the effect (e.g. resolved, done).

message.description: string (optional). Text shown below the icon.

color.description: string (optional). Primary accent color for pulse effect. Default

skeleton_stage_card.description: Dark-themed shimmer skeleton loader for stage use while a playbook is processing. Four variants — card (avatar + text lines), list (avatar rows), media (image header + caption), chat (alternating message bubbles). All use a CSS linear-gradient shimmer animation with no JavaScript.

variant.description: string (optional). One of card, list, media, chat. Default card.

lines.description: integer (optional). Number of text lines or list rows to show. Default 3.

count.description: integer (optional). Number of skeleton cards to stack. Default 1.

marquee_strip.description: Infinite horizontally-scrolling strip of text labels or logo+text items, driven by a CSS @keyframes animation with no JavaScript. Ideal for customer logo rows, tech-stack badges, testimonial tickers, and social proof strips. Pause-on-hover is CSS-only.

items.description: array. Each item is a string or {text, image_url} object.

pause_on_hover.description: bool (optional, default true). Pause scroll on hover.

label.description: string (optional). Small header label above the strip.

gap.description: string (optional). CSS gap between items. Default "40px".

typewriter_text.description: Text that animates itself character by character using a CSS steps() width animation on a monospace element. A blinking cursor is included by default. Useful for hero hooks, slide reveals, and LLM demo showcases. No JavaScript required.

text.description: string. The text to type out.

cursor.description: bool (optional). Show blinking cursor. Default true.

delay.description: string (optional). CSS delay before typing starts. Default "0s".

animated_border_card.description: Card with a continuously rotating gradient border, created by a spinning conic-gradient pseudo-element — no JavaScript. Two accent colours blend through the border. Inner content area has a solid background. Ideal for highlighting key facts, announcements, or featured atoms in a catalogue.

body.description: string. Card body content (markdown inline formatting supported).

accent.description: string (optional). Primary border gradient colour. Default "#38bdf8".

accent2.description: string (optional). Secondary border gradient colour. Default "#818cf8".

background.description: string (optional). Inner card background colour. Default "#ffffff".

border_width.description: integer (optional). Border thickness in px. Default 2.

aurora_background.description: Dark panel with three independently-animating radial gradient blobs that drift and scale using CSS @keyframes — creating an aurora borealis effect with no JavaScript. Supports optional title and body text overlaid on the effect. Ideal for hero sections, cover slides, and atmospheric backgrounds on google-meet-stage.

colors.description: array of up to 3 hex colour strings (optional). Default ["#38bdf8","#818cf8","#34d399"].

title.description: string (optional). Text heading overlaid on the aurora.

body.description: string (optional). Body text overlaid on the aurora (markdown inline supported).

dot_grid_background.description: Panel with a CSS repeating dot, grid, or cross background pattern using background-image with radial-gradient or linear-gradient. No SVG, no JavaScript. Useful as a texture layer behind content in dark or light themes.

title.description: string (optional). Text heading overlaid on the pattern.

body.description: string (optional). Body text overlaid on the pattern (markdown inline supported).

dot_color.description: string (optional). Dot or line colour. Default "rgba(148,163,184,0.35)".

spacing.description: integer (optional). Grid cell size in px. Default 24.

dot_size.description: integer (optional). Dot radius in px (dots variant only). Default 1.

shimmer_button.description: Inline button or link with a diagonal shimmer sweep animation driven by CSS background-position keyframes. The shimmer accent colour sweeps across the button on a loop. No JavaScript. Use for primary CTAs on dark backgrounds.

href.description: string (optional). If set, renders as an anchor tag.

description.description: string (optional). Small text below the button.

card_stack.description: Two to four cards stacked with CSS transform rotate and translateY, creating a fanned deck effect. The front card shows full title and body; back cards are progressively rotated and faded. No JavaScript — purely presentational. Useful for showcasing multiple items with a premium layered feel.

cards.description: array. Each card: {title, body, accent}. Max 4 cards.

height.description: integer (optional). Card height in px. Default 160.

meteor_shower.description: Dark panel with CSS-animated diagonal streaks that fall across the surface like a meteor shower. Each meteor is a rotated span with a linear-gradient tail, animated with staggered delays — no JavaScript, no canvas. Optional title and body text render above the effect layer.

count.description: integer (optional). Number of meteors. Default 20. Max 40.

title.description: string (optional). Text heading overlaid on the effect.

body.description: string (optional). Body text overlaid on the effect (markdown inline supported).

blur_fade_in.description: Container that animates from blurred and transparent to fully visible on mount. Uses CSS @keyframes with filter:blur() and opacity together — no JavaScript. Configurable delay, duration, and direction (up, down, left, right, none).

children.description: array of atom objects to render inside the container (optional).

delay.description: string (optional). CSS delay before animation starts, e.g. "0.3s". Default "0s".

blur.description: string (optional). Starting blur radius, e.g. "8px". Default "8px".

glow_button.description: Button or anchor that uses CSS box-shadow to signal state. Three named states — disabled (grey), ready (brand), fired (success pulse) — each with a distinct glow colour and optional pulse animation. State is set via the state field; no JavaScript required.

href.description: string (optional). If set renders as an anchor tag.

color_ready.description: string (optional). Glow colour in ready state. Default "#38bdf8".

color_fired.description: string (optional). Glow colour in fired state. Default "#34d399".

description.description: string (optional). Small caption text below the button.

animated_beam.description: Inline SVG panel showing two labelled endpoint nodes connected by a path with a pulsing light dot that travels along it, animating via CSS stroke-dashoffset. No JavaScript, no mousemove. Visualises data routing between two named components.

label.description: string (optional). Small caption above the beam diagram.

body.description: string (optional). Description text below the diagram.

active.description: bool (optional). Whether the beam pulse animates. Default true.

color.description: string (optional). Beam and node accent colour. Default "#38bdf8".

curved.description: bool (optional). Use a curved cubic-bezier path. Default true.

encrypted_reveal.description: Text that appears to scramble through random alphanumeric characters before locking into the final readable string. Implemented entirely in CSS using a steps() @keyframes animation cycling through server-side pre-generated scrambled frames — no JavaScript required.

text.description: string. The final text to reveal.

scramble_color.description: string (optional). Colour of scramble characters. Default "#38bdf8".

delay.description: string (optional). CSS delay before reveal starts. Default "0s".

frames.description: integer (optional). Number of scramble frames before lock. Default 8.

word_flip.description: Inline overflow-hidden container that cycles through a list of words using a CSS steps() translateY animation. The agent provides the word array; the component handles all timing and looping. Useful for dynamic hero headlines such as "Grow your [Retention | Revenue | Signups]". No JavaScript required.

words.description: array of strings. The words to cycle through. Minimum 2.

prefix.description: string (optional). Static text before the flipping section, e.g. "Grow your ".

suffix.description: string (optional). Static text after the flipping section.

weight.description: string (optional). Font weight of flipping words. Default "700".

sonar_pulse.description: A visual attention primitive that emits three concentric ring pulses outward from a centre point using staggered CSS @keyframes. Each ring animates independently with a 1-second delay offset, creating a continuous sonar effect. Four named variants map to semantic colours. No JavaScript required.

active.description: bool (optional). If false renders as a static dot. Default true.

label.description: string (optional). Short text label centred in the pulse ring.

body.description: string (optional). Caption text below the pulse ring.

typewriter.description: Text that reveals itself character-by-character using a CSS steps() width animation on an overflow-hidden monospace container, with an optional blinking cursor. No JavaScript required.

text.description: string. The text to type out.

cursor.description: bool (optional). Show blinking cursor after typing. Default true.

number_odometer.description: Slot-machine style digit-by-digit flip animation where each digit column independently scrolls to its target value using staggered CSS translateY keyframes. Distinct from animated_counter which counts up sequentially — this flips each digit column independently.

value.description: string. The target number to display e.g. "1337" or "42.5k".

duration.description: number (optional). Animation duration in seconds. Default 1.2.

typing_indicator.description: Three bouncing dots inside a chat bubble that signals an agent or user is composing a response. Each dot animates with a staggered delay using CSS @keyframes translateY, creating a continuous wave effect.

name.description: string (optional). Agent or user name shown above the bubble. Default "Agent".

countdown_timer.description: Flip-clock style countdown display showing hours, minutes, and seconds in individual digit tiles. Renders a static snapshot of the target time — pair with a dataModelUpdate SSE stream to tick the values.

accent.description: string (optional). Separator and accent colour. Default "#00f2ff".

gradient_text.description: Heading text rendered with an animated shifting gradient fill using CSS background-clip:text and a background-position keyframe. The gradient flows continuously between the from/to colours, creating a shimmer effect on titles and hero text.

text.description: string. The heading text to display.

duration.description: number (optional). Cycle duration in seconds. Default 4.

reveal_on_scroll.description: Content block that starts invisible and fades + slides into view when scrolled into the viewport, using an IntersectionObserver with a CSS class toggle. Requires a small inline script — degrades gracefully to visible static content where JS is unavailable.

title.description: string (optional). Bold heading inside the block.

body.description: string (optional). Body text inside the block.

duration.description: number (optional). Transition duration in seconds. Default 0.7.

accent.description: string (optional). Left border accent colour. Default "#4f46e5".

word_scramble.description: Text that begins as a stream of random alphanumeric characters and progressively resolves left-to-right into the final string using CSS @keyframes content with steps(). Pure CSS — no JavaScript. Each frame pre-generates a partially-revealed scramble via Python at render time.

text.description: string. The final text to reveal.

duration.description: number (optional). Total animation duration in seconds. Default 2.0.

svg_path_draw.description: An SVG path that animates drawing itself from start to end using the stroke-dasharray / stroke-dashoffset CSS technique. The agent selects a named shape (arrow, check, circle, zigzag, infinity) or supplies a custom SVG path string.

width.description: integer (optional). Stroke width in px. Default 3.

duration.description: number (optional). Draw duration in seconds. Default 1.5.

parallax_card.description: A card with CSS perspective and a mousemove JavaScript handler that rotates the card along both axes in response to cursor position, creating a 3D parallax tilt effect. Text layers use translateZ to create depth separation. Requires a minimal inline script.

badge.description: string (optional). Small pill badge above the title.

depth.description: integer (optional). Maximum tilt angle in degrees. Default 15.

quiz_question.description: Multiple-choice or true/false question card with CSS checkbox trick for answer reveal. Correct option is highlighted green on selection, wrong options dim. No JavaScript required — pure CSS :checked selectors drive the feedback state. Optionally shows an explanation block after any answer is chosen.

correct.description: integer. Zero-based index of the correct option.

explanation.description: string (optional). Shown after any option is selected.

fill_in_blank.description: Cloze-test exercise where one or more blanks in a sentence or paragraph are replaced by inline input fields. On submission a minimal inline script compares trimmed lowercase input against accepted answers and highlights each blank green (correct) or red (wrong). A retry button resets all inputs.

template.description: string. Sentence with {blank} placeholders marking each gap.

answers.description: list[string | list[string]]. Accepted answer(s) per blank in order. A list entry may itself be a list of acceptable alternatives.

hint.description: string (optional). Shown below the exercise as a nudge.

match_exercise.description: Click-to-pair matching exercise. Two columns of items are shown — left (terms) and right (definitions). Learner clicks a term then a definition to form a pair. Matched pairs lock and highlight green; a mismatch briefly flashes red then resets. A score chip shows pairs matched / total. Driven by a minimal inline script.

pairs.description: list[{term: string, definition: string}]. 3 to 8 pairs recommended.

shuffle.description: boolean (optional, default true). Randomise right-column order on render.

hint_reveal.description: A "Show hint" disclosure button that expands to reveal help text. Built on the HTML details/summary element — no JavaScript required. The summary label toggles between "Show hint" and "Hide hint". Styled with a subtle left-border accent and muted background to visually separate it from body content.

hint.description: string. The hint text revealed on expand.

accent.description: string (optional). Left-border and icon colour. Default "#6366f1".

achievement_badge.description: Unlockable achievement or completion badge displayed as a circular icon with a title, description, and optional unlock date. Locked state shows a greyscale padlock overlay. Suitable for course completion, streak milestones, and skill certifications. Renders as a centred card or inline pill depending on size variant.

icon.description: string (optional). Emoji or single character used as the badge icon. Default "🏆".

locked.description: boolean (optional, default false). Shows greyscale padlock overlay when true.

unlocked_at.description: string (optional). ISO date string shown beneath the badge when unlocked.

score_summary.description: End-of-exercise or end-of-module score card showing correct answers, total questions, percentage, and optional time taken. Animates the score fraction on render using a CSS counter. Includes a contextual pass/fail or grade label and an optional retry/continue CTA row.

pass_threshold.description: integer (optional). Percentage (0–100) required to pass. Drives pass/fail label colour.

retry_label.description: string (optional). Label for the retry button. Omit to hide retry CTA.

continue_label.description: string (optional). Label for the continue button. Omit to hide continue CTA.

continue_url.description: url (optional). Destination for the continue button.

xp_bar.description: Experience points or gamification progress bar showing current XP, level label, and XP needed to reach the next level. The fill animates from the previous value to the current value on render via CSS transition. A level-up flash overlay triggers when xp_current equals or exceeds xp_next.

level_label.description: string. Current level name e.g. "Level 3 — Intermediate".

xp_current.description: integer. Current XP within the current level.

xp_next.description: integer. XP required to reach the next level.

show_flash.description: boolean (optional, default true). Trigger level-up flash when xp_current >= xp_next.

lesson_nav.description: Previous/next lesson navigation bar with the current lesson title centred and module context shown above. Each side shows the adjacent lesson title and a directional arrow. Optionally shows a completion checkbox for the current lesson that persists via localStorage.

module_label.description: string (optional). Module or section name shown above the nav bar.

current_title.description: string. Title of the current lesson.

prev_title.description: string (optional). Title of the previous lesson. Omit to hide prev arrow.

prev_url.description: url (optional). Link for the previous lesson.

next_title.description: string (optional). Title of the next lesson. Omit to hide next arrow.

next_url.description: url (optional). Link for the next lesson.

show_completion.description: boolean (optional, default false). Show a "Mark as complete" checkbox persisted in localStorage.

course_progress_card.description: Overall course completion card showing a list of modules with individual progress bars and a top-level aggregate progress ring. Each module row shows its title, lesson count, and percentage complete. Suitable for a course sidebar or dashboard widget.

course_title.description: string. Course name shown at the top of the card.

modules.description: list[{title: string, lessons_total: integer, lessons_done: integer}].

highlighted_text.description: Inline passage of text with a coloured highlight background and an optional margin annotation note. The annotation appears as a side-note pill on hover (CSS :hover) or as a visible aside on smaller viewports. Useful for reading comprehension exercises, exam technique callouts, and annotated source analysis.

annotation.description: string (optional). Margin note revealed on hover.

drive_file_list.description: Live list of files in a Google Drive folder rendered with icons and download links. Works on Google Apps Script only.

folder_id.description: string. The Google Drive folder ID to list files from.

max_results.description: integer (optional). Maximum number of files to return. Default is 10.

sheet_preview.description: Live read-only preview of a specified range in a Google Sheet. Renders as an HTML table. Works on Google Apps Script only.

sheet_name.description: string. The name of the sheet tab.

range.description: string. The A1 notation range (e.g. A1:D10).

gmail_summary.description: Summary list of recent emails in the user's Gmail matching a search query. Works on Google Apps Script only.

max_results.description: integer (optional). Maximum number of emails to display. Default is 5.

calendar_upcoming.description: List of upcoming events from the user's primary calendar. Works on Google Apps Script only.

max_results.description: integer (optional). Maximum number of upcoming events to show. Default is 5.

user_greeting.description: Personalised greeting showing the active user's Google account email. Works on Google Apps Script only.

prefix.description: string (optional). The greeting prefix. Default is "Hello".

script_run_button.description: Interactive button that executes a custom Google Apps Script server function when clicked, showing a loading spinner and returning/displaying the function result. Works on Google Apps Script only.

function_name.description: string. The name of the server-side V8 JavaScript function to call.

argument.description: string (optional). Optional string argument to pass to the function.

calendar_today.description: Today's events from the user's default Google Calendar, displayed as a compact timeline with time and location. Automatically shows today's date in the header.

title.description: string (optional). Card heading. Default is "Today's Schedule".

max_results.description: integer (optional). Maximum events to show. Default 8.

sheet_stats.description: Aggregate statistics (sum, average, count, min, max) computed from a Google Sheet range and displayed as a row of stat badges. Ideal for dashboards.

label.description: string (optional). Card heading. Default is "Sheet Stats".

show.description: array (optional). Which stats to show — any of sum, average, count, min, max. Default ["sum","average","count"].

accent.description: string (optional). Accent colour for stat values.

gmail_unread_count.description: Unread message count badges for one or more Gmail labels, shown as a pill row. Zero counts are displayed in grey; non-zero in the accent colour.

labels.description: array (optional). Gmail label names to count. Default ["INBOX"].

accent.description: string (optional). Badge colour for non-zero counts. Default red.

counts.description: object (static connector). Label→count map for non-GAS surfaces.

auth_token.description: string (api connector). OAuth2 bearer token for REST API.

user_profile_card.description: Personalised profile card showing the active user's avatar initial, display name (derived from email), email address, and Workspace domain. On GAS uses Session.getActiveUser(); on other surfaces renders from name/email fields.

name.description: string (static connector). Display name for non-GAS surfaces.

email.description: string (static connector). Email address for non-GAS surfaces.

subtitle.description: string (optional). Role or team label shown below the email.

drive_storage_usage.description: Google Drive storage quota displayed as a labelled progress bar — used GB, total GB, and percentage. Bar colour shifts to amber above 70% and red above 90%. On GAS uses DriveApp; elsewhere pass used_gb and total_gb fields.

label.description: string (optional). Card heading. Default is "Drive Storage".

used_gb.description: number (static connector). GB used — for non-GAS surfaces.

total_gb.description: number (static connector). Total quota GB — for non-GAS surfaces.

auth_token.description: string (api connector). OAuth2 bearer token for REST API.

sheet_form_submit.description: Inline form that appends a timestamped row to a Google Sheet on submit. Calls google.script.run.a2uiSheetAppend server-side. Each field renders as a labelled input or textarea.

sheet_name.description: string (optional). Sheet tab name. Default is "Sheet1".

fields.description: array. Array of {label, name, type, placeholder} objects. type is text, email, number, or textarea.

submit_label.description: string (optional). Submit button text. Default is "Submit".

tasks_today.description: Incomplete tasks displayed as an interactive checkbox list. On GAS uses the Tasks API for live data; on other surfaces renders from an items[] array or the Google Tasks REST API.

title.description: string (optional). Card heading. Default is "Today's Tasks".

max_results.description: integer (optional). Maximum tasks to show. Default 10.

list_name.description: string (optional, gas-native). Name of the task list.

items.description: array (static connector). Array of {title, due, completed} for non-GAS surfaces.

auth_token.description: string (api connector). OAuth2 bearer token for REST API.

cursor_glow.description: Ambient radial gradient orb that smoothly lerp-follows the cursor across the page using requestAnimationFrame. Uses CSS mix-blend-mode:screen for a light-leak feel. Page-scoped and double-init guarded.

size.description: integer (optional). Orb diameter in px. Default 380.

speed.description: number (optional). Lerp factor 0–1 — lower = more lag. Default 0.1.

cursor_trail.description: A chain of fading dots that follow the cursor using worm-chain physics — each dot lerps toward the one ahead of it, creating a fluid trailing effect.

length.description: integer (optional). Number of chain dots. Default 16.

size.description: integer (optional). Lead dot diameter px. Default 10.

speed.description: number (optional). Lerp factor per dot. Default 0.35.

particle_burst.description: On every click anywhere on the page, coloured particles burst outward from the click point with random velocities and simulated gravity, then fade out.

colours.description: array (optional). Array of hex colour strings.

spotlight_cursor.description: A dark overlay with a soft-edged circular cutout that follows the cursor — torch or spotlight effect. Best used as a standalone page effect or dramatic opening slide.

soft_edge.description: integer (optional). Feather distance px beyond radius. Default 60.

magnetic_element.description: Wraps content that drifts toward the cursor when it enters the activation radius, then springs back with a cubic-bezier overshoot when the cursor leaves.

label.description: string (optional). Text label for the default pill style.

content.description: string (optional). Raw HTML to use instead of the default pill.

tilt_card.description: A card that tilts in 3D perspective as the cursor moves across it, with a radial glare highlight that tracks cursor position. Uses CSS preserve-3d and dynamic box-shadow.

content.description: string (optional). Inner HTML rendered inside the card.

title.description: string (optional). Card heading rendered above content.

glare.description: boolean (optional). Show glare highlight. Default true.

doc_ai_summary.description: Opens a Google Doc by ID, sends its text to Vertex AI Gemini, and renders the response as a structured summary card with word count and a link to the original doc. Requires VERTEX_PROJECT_ID in Script Properties.

doc_id.description: string. Google Doc ID to summarise.

prompt.description: string (optional). Instruction sent to Gemini before the doc text.

title.description: string (optional). Override card title. Defaults to the doc name.

model.description: string (optional). Gemini model override. Defaults to VERTEX_MODEL property.

max_chars.description: integer (optional). Max doc characters sent to Gemini. Default 12000.

show_meta.description: boolean (optional). Show word count and doc link. Default true.

multi_doc_ai_brief.description: Takes an array of Google Doc IDs and renders one Vertex AI Gemini summary card per doc — a compact briefing pack for meetings. Each doc can have its own prompt override.

docs.description: array. Array of {doc_id, title?, prompt?} objects.

default_prompt.description: string (optional). Fallback prompt for docs without their own.

accent.description: string (optional). Accent colour for doc links.

gradient_heading.description: Gradient-fill standalone heading using CSS background-clip text. Simpler than dark_hero — just the text, no padding or CTA. Use inside content flow between other atoms.

gradient.description: string (optional). CSS gradient value. Default indigo→violet→pink.

display_quote.description: Large typographic quote with a decorative quotation mark at 5rem, quote body in italic, and optional attribution line. Dark-native, great for Meet Stage title slides.

attribution.description: string (optional). Name or source shown below quote.

colour.description: string (optional). Accent colour for mark and attribution. Default

size.description: string (optional). Font-size for quote text. Default clamp(1.4rem,3vw,2.2rem).

align.description: string (optional). center or left. Default center.

split_stat.description: Two-column layout — a large glowing stat number on the left (with neon text-shadow) and heading + body text on the right. Classic keynote layout, great for Meet Stage.

value.description: string. The stat number or short value.

flip.description: boolean (optional). Put stat on right side. Default false.

word_reveal.description: Words appear one by one with a fade-up animation that auto-plays on page load — no user interaction required. Great for Meet Stage title slides and reveal moments.

text.description: string. Text to reveal word by word.

gradient.description: string (optional). CSS gradient applied to whole line (overrides colour).

delay.description: number (optional). Seconds between each word. Default 0.12.

section_label.description: Uppercase section marker with a short glowing accent line — use between content sections on dark pages to create visual rhythm.

count_up_stat.description: A stat number that counts up from zero to the target value using a cubic ease function on page load. Auto-plays — no user interaction required.

value.description: integer. Target number to count to.

label.description: string (optional). Descriptor shown below the number.

text_highlight.description: Inline prose sentence where specific words are highlighted in an accent colour. Mark words with **double asterisks** in the text field — highlighted words are wrapped in a styled span.

text.description: string. Body text with **highlighted** words wrapped in double asterisks.

reveal_line.description: A single line of text that sweeps in from left using a clip-path animation — dramatic and instant. Auto-plays on load, no click required. Great for Meet Stage opening slides.

gradient.description: string (optional). CSS gradient applied to text. Default indigo→pink.

canvas_plexus.description: Full-viewport animated particle network — dots connected by proximity lines that fade with distance. Mouse/pointer repulses particles. Zero external dependencies, pure requestAnimationFrame loop.

colour.description: string (optional). Hex colour for particles and lines. Default

max_dist.description: integer (optional). Max distance px for edge connections. Default 120.

repulse_radius.description: integer (optional). Mouse repulsion radius px. Default 90.

spring_nodes.description: Interactive mass-spring physics simulation — nodes repel each other (Coulomb) and are held by Hooke springs on defined edges. Click and drag to pin and move nodes. Settles naturally from a stacked initial position.

nodes.description: array (optional). Array of {id, label} objects. Defaults to 6 labelled nodes.

edges.description: array (optional). Array of {a, b} index pairs for spring connections.

colour.description: string (optional). Node and edge accent colour. Default

rest_length.description: integer (optional). Spring rest length px. Default 110.

isometric_mesh.description: Rotating 3D isometric height-field mesh. Default surface is a 16×16 Gaussian hill pre-computed server-side. Drag to rotate manually; auto-rotates until first touch. Height colour interpolates from deep indigo (low) to bright violet (high).

colour_low.description: string (optional). Hex colour at minimum height. Default

colour_high.description: string (optional). Hex colour at maximum height. Default

auto_rotate.description: boolean (optional). Auto-rotate until user drags. Default true.

geo_iso_takeoff.description: Full-viewport isometric 3D canvas animation of an A321neo (or other narrowbody) departing from Toulouse Blagnac (LFBO). Shows rolling takeoff run, rotation, gear retraction, and climb-out against a night-sky grid. HUD overlay shows airline code, aircraft type, and runway. Accent colour auto-selected from airline ICAO (AIB=Airbus blue, EZY=orange, AFR=Air France blue, BAW=British Airways red, DLH=Lufthansa navy, RYR=Ryanair blue). Includes a LIVE RADAR button that opens the airspace_command_deck for LFBO.

title.description: string (optional). HUD title line. Default "LFBO RWY 32L — A321neo DEPARTURE".

airline.description: string (optional). ICAO airline code for accent colour — AIB, EZY, AFR, BAW, DLH, RYR. Default AIB.

aircraft_type.description: string (optional). ICAO aircraft type code — A21N (A321neo), A320, B738. Default A21N.

geo_iso_rocket_launch.description: Full-viewport isometric 3D canvas animation of an Ariane 6 rocket launch from Kourou ELA-4. Shows pad, ignition, liftoff, and ascent trajectory. HUD overlay with mission data.

title.description: string (optional). HUD title. Default "KOUROU ELA-4 — HEAVY LIFT INJECTION PROFILE".

vehicle.description: string (optional). ARIANE_6 or ARIANE_5. Default ARIANE_6.

geo_iso_heli_hover.description: Full-viewport isometric 3D canvas animation of an H160 helicopter hovering over a helipad. Rotor blur, downwash ripple, and HUD overlay with livery accent colour.

title.description: string (optional). HUD title. Default "LFBO HELIPAD 2 — AIRBUS H160 HOVER PROFILE".

livery.description: string (optional). ICAO airline code for accent colour — AIB, EZY, AFR, etc. Default AIB.

geo_iso_fleet.description: Full-viewport tabbed showcase of all three isometric aviation animations — A321neo takeoff, Ariane 6 launch, and H160 hover — in a single atom with tab switcher. Use this for a full aviation/aerospace demo page. tab field sets which scene opens by default.

airline.description: string (optional). Airline ICAO for A321neo accent — AIB, EZY, AFR, BAW, DLH, RYR. Default AIB.

rocket.description: string (optional). ESA or CNES. Default ESA.

livery.description: string (optional). Airline ICAO for H160 livery. Default AIB.

airspace_command_deck.description: Full-viewport Toulouse TMA airspace radar display — canvas radar with animated simulated flights, rotating sweep, VOR beacons, and approach paths. HTML/CSS overlay for crisp chyron title, weather panel, flight list HUD, and scrolling ticker. Driven by the toulouse_airspace.yaml playbook via the ?slide= URL parameter. For the Toulouse demo, prefer routing through Code.js _renderAirspaceSlide() which handles live METAR interpolation.

chyron_subtitle.description: string (optional). Top-left subtitle — supports interpolated METAR tags.

ticker_speed.description: integer (optional). Ticker scroll speed px/s. Default 45.

panel_type.description: string (optional). supervisor | target | (empty). Controls HUD flight list panel.

lockedCallsign.description: string (optional). Highlight and add targeting reticle to this callsign.

zoom.description: integer (optional). TMA radius shown in nm. Default 35.

show_slate.description: boolean (optional). Render calibration boot slate instead of radar.

poll_question.description: string (optional). If set, renders a vote overlay with bar chart.

poll_options.description: array (optional). Array of option strings for the poll.

poll_values.description: array (optional). Array of integer vote counts for each option.

data_source.description: Generic HTTP GET data feed. Fetches on server-side render (GAS surface via UrlFetchApp) then refreshes client-side via google.script.run.fetchDataSource(). Publishes to window.A2UI_DATA[name] and calls window.A2UI_CALLBACKS[name]. Other surfaces would use fetch() or requests instead.

name.description: string. Feed identifier — other atoms subscribe to this name (required).

path.description: string (optional). Dot-notation path into parsed response (e.g. data.items).

refresh.description: integer (optional). Client refresh interval seconds. 0 = initial load only. Default 0.

cache.description: integer (optional). Server-side CacheService TTL seconds. Default 15.

adsb_feed.description: OpenSky Network ADS-B live traffic feed for a geographic bounding box. Fetches server-side on render (cached), refreshes client-side every refresh seconds. Publishes normalised flight objects {callsign, lat, lon, alt_ft, spd_kt, hdg, on_ground, squawk} to the named feed. Unauthenticated OpenSky limit is 400 req/day — keep cache TTL >= 15s.

name.description: string (optional). Feed name other atoms subscribe to. Default adsb.

lat_min.description: number (optional). Bounding box south edge degrees. Default 43.1 (LFBO TMA).

lat_max.description: number (optional). Bounding box north edge degrees. Default 44.2.

lon_min.description: number (optional). Bounding box west edge degrees. Default 0.7.

lon_max.description: number (optional). Bounding box east edge degrees. Default 2.0.

refresh.description: integer (optional). Client refresh interval seconds. Default 15.

filter_ground.description: boolean (optional). Exclude on-ground traffic. Default true.

cache.description: integer (optional). Server-side cache TTL seconds. Default 15.

metar_feed.description: METAR weather for an ICAO station via aviationweather.gov. Fetches server-side on render (cached), refreshes client-side every refresh seconds. Publishes {wind, temp, qnh, raw} to the named feed. Visual atoms bind to it by name and receive updates without a page reload.

name.description: string (optional). Feed name other atoms subscribe to. Default metar.

station.description: string (optional). ICAO station code. Default LFBO.

refresh.description: integer (optional). Client refresh interval seconds. Default 60.

cache.description: integer (optional). Server-side cache TTL seconds. Default 30.

firestore_read.description: Data connector: reads a single Firestore document (noun: document at project/collection/doc_id) on server-side render (verb: read) and publishes the deserialized result to window.A2UI_DATA[name]. Visual atoms subscribe via window.A2UI_CALLBACKS[name] — the named connector wires data to presentation without direct coupling. GAS surface fetches via Firestore REST API using ScriptApp.getOAuthToken(); requires the datastore OAuth scope in appsscript.json. Optional client-side refresh calls google.script.run.fetchFirestoreDoc() so the page stays live without a reload. Data shape is surface-agnostic — any visual atom can bind to the named feed.

name.description: string. Connector name — visual atoms subscribe via window.A2UI_CALLBACKS[name] (required).

project.description: string. GCP project ID that owns the Firestore database (required).

doc_id.description: string. Document ID within the collection (required).

refresh.description: integer (optional). Client-side refresh interval seconds. 0 = initial load only. Default 0.

paragraph.description: Prose paragraph block (alias for body, google-apps-script-web only)

text_block.description: Plain text block rendered as a paragraph

blockquote.description: Styled pull-quote with optional attribution line

height.description: string or number (optional, CSS height e.g. 24 or "2rem")

badge.description: Inline badge pill with accent colour

link_button.description: Text hyperlink styled as a button

cta_button.description: Full-width call-to-action button linking to a URL

nav_link.description: Single CTA button or link that navigates to a named A2UI page. Automatically appends &from=<current_slug> so the destination page's back button returns here. Style variants — primary (filled indigo), ghost (border), or text (minimal).

nav_slug.description: string (required). Slug of the destination named page.

info_card.description: Simple card with title and body text

ai_build_trace.description: Token usage and model trace card — shows Gemini call stats inline on a page

gemini_prompt.description: Inline Gemini prompt input that calls the GAS callGemini() function from within a rendered page

dark_hero.description: Full-width dark hero banner with gradient background, badge, headline, and CTA button

glowing_stat.description: Single large stat with neon glow effect on dark background

glass_card.description: Frosted-glass card with backdrop-filter blur. Use for dark-theme highlight boxes, callouts, or content wrappers. Content is raw HTML string.

content.description: string (optional). Inner HTML content of the card.

blur.description: integer (optional). Backdrop blur in px. Default 18.

gradient_border_card.description: Card with an animated gradient border stroke on dark background

colours.description: array of hex strings (optional, gradient stops)

floating_orbs.description: Decorative blurred colour orbs that float in a dark background area

neon_text.description: Neon-coloured glowing text, ideal for dark technical pages

dark_feature_grid.description: Responsive feature grid on dark background — icon, title, description per cell

dark_divider.description: Thin horizontal rule for dark theme pages

ambient_gradient.description: Full-screen animated aurora background — alias for aurora_background

depth_stack.description: Stacked layered cards with parallax depth — alias for card_stack

scramble_reveal.description: Text that decrypts/un-scrambles character-by-character on scroll — alias for encrypted_reveal

scroll_trigger.description: Content that animates in from a direction when scrolled into view — alias for reveal_on_scroll

flow_connector.description: Animated dashed beam connecting two labelled nodes — alias for animated_beam

live_metric.description: Animated counter that counts up from start to a target value — alias for animated_counter

deadline_ticker.description: Countdown timer showing days/hours/minutes/seconds to a target date — alias for countdown_timer

pattern_background.description: Dot-grid repeating pattern background — alias for dot_grid_background

glitch_text.description: Cyan glitch-effect headline — periodic RGB-split animation

neon_glow.description: Pulsing neon-glow text on a near-black background

magnetic_button.description: Button that follows the cursor magnetically on hover

confetti_trigger.description: Button that triggers confetti explosion when clicked

label.description: string (message shown after click, alias: text)

floating_particles.description: Floating particle field background (canvas fallback placeholder)

scroll_progress.description: Thin fixed progress bar at the top of the viewport showing scroll position

live_clock.description: Live ticking clock on a dark card, updates every second

timezone.description: string (optional, IANA tz label for display only)

decision_tree.description: Interactive collapsible decision tree with nested branches

nodes.description: array of {text, children: [{text, children}]} — nested tree nodes

step_reveal_sequence.description: Tab-style step-by-step content with radio-button navigation

steps.description: array of {title, text} — each step shown in its own tab panel

chat_sequence.description: Animated conversation thread with user/assistant bubbles

tooltip_glossary.description: Glossary list where hovering a term shows its definition as a tooltip

focus_lens.description: Blurred background with a clear focused panel in the centre — spotlight effect

terminal_boot.description: Dark terminal window that types out boot/log lines one by one

lines.description: array of strings — lines typed out in sequence

stagger_list.description: List of items that animate in with a staggered delay — supports icons and descriptions

items.description: array of strings or {icon, text, sub} objects

stagger.description: number (optional, delay between items in seconds, default 0.1)

liquid_button.description: Button that morphs between circular and rounded-rectangle on hover

highlight_sweep.description: Text with an animated highlight sweep underline that plays on load

color.description: string (optional, highlight colour hex, default #fef08a yellow)

progress_reveal.description: Animated progress bar that counts from 0 to a value when scrolled into view

big_reveal.description: Large centred number or word that pops in with a spring scale animation

text.description: string (the big value, alias: value, label)

kinetic_headline.description: Headline where each word flies in with a staggered animation

text_reveal_mask.description: Headline that sweeps in from left using clip-path mask animation

split_reveal.description: Content hidden behind two coloured panels that slide apart to reveal it

mesh_gradient.description: Dark card with radial mesh/blob gradient background — vivid multi-colour

colors.description: array of hex strings (optional, gradient blob colours)

stripe_background.description: Card with animated diagonal stripe background pattern

counter_group.description: Row of animated counting stats separated by dividers

stats.description: array of {value, label, color (optional), prefix (optional), suffix (optional), decimals (optional)} (alias: items)

orbit_diagram.description: Animated orbiting node diagram — central node with satellite labels on a dashed orbit ring

nodes.description: array of strings or {label, color} objects

noise_card.description: Dark card with subtle SVG film-grain noise texture overlay

comparison_morph.description: Draggable before/after slider revealing two text panels side by side

word_cloud.description: Interactive word cloud — static words or live Google Sheets feed with submit input

palette.description: array of hex strings (optional, word colours)

accent.description: string (optional, hex accent for input bar)

sheet_url.description: string (optional, Google Sheet CSV URL for live words)

write_url.description: string (optional, GAS doGet URL to write submissions)

quiz_set.description: Multi-question quiz with multiple-choice options, per-question explanations, pass score, and result screen

questions.description: array of {question, options: [string], correct: integer (0-based index), explanation (optional)}
