on_pass.description: object (optional, atom block shown on pass)

on_fail.description: object (optional, atom block shown on fail)

globe_3d.description: Spinning interactive 3-D wireframe or earth globe rendered on HTML5 canvas — draggable with inertia

dots.description: array of {lat, lon, label (optional), color (optional)} (optional, pins on globe)

arcs.description: array of {from: [lat,lon], to: [lat,lon], color (optional)} (optional, great-circle arcs)

geo_mercator_radar.description: Interactive Mercator projection map with draggable pan, node pins, and animated link vectors — dark radar aesthetic

nodes.description: array of {id, name, lat, lon, value (optional)}

geo_contour_waves.description: Animated isobaric atmospheric contour wave field — fluid procedural sine/cosine curves on dark background

intensity.description: number (optional, wave band count, default 4, max 8)

multi_surface.description: One data pool rendered simultaneously across three surface engines: spatial map (desktop), phone frame card list (mobile), circular focus face (IoT/watch)

nodes.description: array of {id, type, label, temp (optional), value (optional), intensity (0–100, optional), coords: {x, y}}

geo_europe_airspace.description: Interactive SVG European airspace map — country outlines, airport pins, simulated flight tracks, click-to-open TMA playbook

focus.description: string (optional, country name to highlight, alias: country)

sim_flights.description: array of {callsign, from: [lat,lon], to: [lat,lon]} (optional simulated flight tracks)

airports.description: boolean (optional, show airport pins, default true)

feed_status.description: Live-feed status pill — shows LIVE (n) or SIM based on data published to a named feed

playbook.description: Full-screen multi-slide presentation deck — each slide is its own atom block array, with shared data feeds and slide navigation

slides.description: array of {id (optional), blocks: [atom block objects]} — each slide is a full block list

shared_blocks.description: array of atom block objects with role:data_source (optional). Rendered once before slides. Use for adsb_feed, metar_feed, firestore_read etc. — atoms that publish to window.A2UI_CALLBACKS[name] and have no visual output. Visual atoms in slides bind by name via data_source / weather_source / connector fields.

surface_unlocked.description: Game-style "NEW SURFACE UNLOCKED" achievement notification card

sub.description: string (optional, subtitle, default NEW SURFACE UNLOCKED)

schema_reveal.description: Decodes the current page ?p= URL and displays the JSON schema that built this page

url_anatomy.description: Visually annotates the current renderer URL, highlighting the GAS endpoint, ?p= parameter, and encoded payload

schema_qr.description: QR code for the current page URL (or a fixed URL) via Google Charts API

url.description: string (optional, fixed URL — defaults to current page URL)

take_away_card.description: Bold single-insight pull-quote card designed to be screenshotted

gradient.description: array of 2 hex strings (optional, background gradient colours)

next_step_strip.description: Horizontal strip of numbered next steps with optional links

steps.description: array of {number (optional), label, detail (optional, alias: action), url (optional)}

copy_prompt.description: Monospace copyable text block — ideal for sharing Gemini prompts or code

prompt.description: string (text to display and copy, alias: text, content)

atom_anatomy.description: Side-by-side panel showing a rendered atom alongside its raw JSON schema

schema.description: object (atom block JSON to render and display)

renderer_stats.description: Simple stat grid showing custom key/value pairs — used to show renderer capabilities

prompt_to_schema.description: Three-panel flow diagram: natural language prompt → generated JSON schema → rendered page

schema.description: object or string (the generated JSON schema)

before_after_stack.description: Animated comparison: old approach items cross out one by one, then new approach slides in

items.description: array of strings (old approach items to cross out, alias: before_items)

result.description: string (the new approach shown after animation, alias: after_text)

live_vote.description: Live audience voting: buttons to vote, bar chart results, optional Google Sheets backend

sheet_url.description: string (optional, Google Sheet CSV for live tallies)

write_url.description: string (optional, GAS doGet URL to record votes)

reaction_shower.description: Emoji reaction buttons — tapping rains the emoji down the screen, with optional Sheet-backed totals

write_url.description: string (optional, GAS doGet URL to record reactions)

sheet_url.description: string (optional, Google Sheet CSV for live counts)

raise_hand.description: Audience raise-hand button with live count — optional Google Sheets backend

label.description: string (optional, button label, default ✋ Raise hand)

write_url.description: string (optional, GAS doGet URL to record hands)

sheet_url.description: string (optional, Google Sheet CSV for live count)

surface_map.description: Visual diagram showing the A2UI surfaces — schema feeds into each render target

surfaces.description: array of {name, icon, desc, color} (optional, defaults to GAS/Meet/Sites/Chat)

speed_counter.description: Stopwatch that starts ticking on load and freezes when the page finishes loading — shows real page load time

live_edit.description: Embedded mini schema editor with a textarea for typing a single atom JSON block and a live rendered preview below

renderer_url.description: string (optional, renderer base URL for linking)

gmail_inbox.description: Horizontal swipeable carousel of Gmail inbox threads. Each card shows a coloured sender avatar, name, subject (bold if unread), snippet and timestamp. Clicking opens the thread in Gmail. On GAS uses GmailApp for live data; on other surfaces renders from items[] array or Gmail REST API.

title.description: string (optional). Section label above carousel. Default "Inbox".

count.description: integer (optional). Number of threads, max 20. Default 10.

accent.description: string (optional). Accent colour for unread indicator and nav dot. Default

items.description: array (static connector). Email objects for non-GAS surfaces.

auth_token.description: string (api connector). OAuth2 bearer token for REST API.

drive_recent_files.description: Horizontal swipeable carousel of recently modified Drive files. Each card shows a coloured file-type badge (DOC/XLS/PPT/PDF), name, relative modified time, and owner. On GAS searches files modified in last 30 days via DriveApp; on other surfaces renders from items[] array or Drive REST API.

title.description: string (optional). Section label above carousel. Default "Recent Files".

count.description: integer (optional). Number of files to show, max 20. Default 10.

accent.description: string (optional). Accent colour for active nav dot. Default

items.description: array (static connector). File objects for non-GAS surfaces.

auth_token.description: string (api connector). OAuth2 bearer token for REST API.

drive_folder_contents.description: Responsive grid of files and subfolders inside a Drive folder. Subfolders appear first, each item shows a coloured file-type badge and name, clicking opens in Drive. On GAS uses DriveApp with folder_id; on other surfaces renders from items[] array or Drive REST API with folder_id + auth_token.

folder_id.description: string (gas-native/api). The Google Drive folder ID to browse.

title.description: string (optional). Override for the folder name shown as header.

count.description: integer (optional). Max items to show. Default 12.

items.description: array (static connector). File objects for non-GAS surfaces.

auth_token.description: string (api connector). OAuth2 bearer token for REST API.

drive_file_card.description: Single Drive file card — coloured file-type badge (DOC/XLS/PPT/PDF), file name, optional description, and an "Open →" button. Works on any surface using static name/mime/url fields. On GAS, file_id triggers live lookup via DriveApp to populate name and mime automatically.

file_id.description: string (gas-native). Drive file ID for live name/type lookup on GAS.

name.description: string. File display name — required on non-GAS surfaces.

mime.description: string. MIME type for badge colour (e.g. application/vnd.google-apps.spreadsheet).

url.description: string. URL to open — required on non-GAS surfaces.

description.description: string (optional). Short description shown below the file name.

progress_store.description: Invisible state connector atom — no visual output. Initialises window._A2UI_STORE and dispatches "a2ui:store" CustomEvent. On GAS reads initial state from a Google Sheet at render time and writes back async via google.script.run. On web reads/writes localStorage keyed by course_id. All other LMS atoms read from and write to this shared store. Must appear before any stateful atom on the page.

course_id.description: string (required). Unique course identifier — scopes the Sheet and localStorage key.

module_map.description: Visual curriculum grid showing all modules in a course. Each card displays module icon, title, description, duration, and a live status badge (locked/Start/Done) driven by progress_store. Locked modules are greyed out. Completed modules turn green. Cards navigate to module url on click.

modules.description: array (required). Array of module objects. Each module supports: id (string, required), title, description, icon, duration, lessons, required[] (array of module ids that must be complete to unlock), and either: page (array of atom blocks, PREFERRED — auto-encoded into a self-contained URL at render time, no separate save needed) or url (string — only for external URLs or pre-saved nav pages). Always use page for inline module content.

knowledge_check.description: Lightweight inline comprehension pulse — a single multiple-choice question with instant feedback and no score impact. Correct answer turns green with explanation; wrong answer turns red with explanation. All options lock after selection. No retry gate — learner simply continues reading.

correct.description: integer (required). Zero-based index of the correct answer.

explanation.description: string (optional). Explanation shown after any selection.

quiz_result_summary.description: End-of-quiz result screen showing score percentage, pass/fail badge, time taken, per-question dot breakdown (green tick / red cross), and navigation buttons. Automatically writes the score to progress_store keyed by quiz_id. Pass mark is configurable.

quiz_id.description: string (required). ID used to write score to progress_store.

pass_mark.description: integer (optional). Percentage required to pass. Default 70.

time_secs.description: integer (optional). Time taken in seconds for display.

questions.description: array (optional). Array of {label, correct} for per-question breakdown dots.

next_url.description: string (optional). URL for Continue button (shown when passed).

scenario_branch.description: Narrative branching learning atom. Presents a real-world situation with 2-4 labelled choices. Each choice reveals a consequence with good/neutral/bad outcome styling (green/amber/red). Includes a "try again" reset on non-linked consequences. Ideal for compliance, leadership, and soft-skills training.

scenario.description: string (required). The situation or challenge presented to the learner.

context.description: string (optional). Background context shown in italics above the scenario.

accent.description: string (optional). Accent colour for choice buttons. Default

choices.description: array (required). Array of {label, consequence, outcome, next_url?} objects. outcome is "good", "neutral", or "bad".

completion_gate.description: Renders a locked placeholder card until the specified module id is marked complete in progress_store. Once complete, the gate disappears to reveal content below it. Use to sequence content and prevent skipping.

requires.description: string (required). Module id that must be complete in progress_store to unlock.

message.description: string (optional). Message shown on the lock card.

certification_card.description: Completion certificate card — earner name, course title, issuer, and date with a gradient border and trophy icon. Locked behind a requires check against progress_store; becomes visible when the course is marked complete. On GAS, earner name is derived from active session if not supplied.

course.description: string (required). Course name on the certificate.

issuer.description: string (optional). Issuing organisation name. Default "A2UI Learning".

requires.description: string (optional). Module/course id that must be complete to reveal the certificate.

earner.description: string (optional). Earner display name — auto-derived from GAS session if not set.

date.description: string (optional). Completion date string. Defaults to today.

skill_radar.description: SVG spider/radar chart plotting learner competency levels across multiple skill dimensions. Optional before values render as a dashed polygon overlay for before/after growth comparison. Values are 0-100 per skill.

accent.description: string (optional). Fill/stroke colour for current polygon. Default

skills.description: array (required). Array of {label, value (0-100), before? (0-100)} objects.

badge_showcase.description: Achievement badge wall — all course badges displayed in a grid. Earned badges glow with indigo highlight and full colour emoji; locked badges are greyed and dimmed. Each badge checks its required_id against progress_store to determine earned status.

badges.description: array (required). Array of {id, label, icon, description, required_id} objects. required_id is the progress_store key to check for completion.

learning_path_selector.description: Path chooser presented at course entry. Shows 2-4 role or level path cards (icon, title, description, duration). Learner clicks to select — selection is stored in progress_store via setPath() and optionally navigates to a path-specific url. Selected path can drive personalised module_map content.

title.description: string (optional). Chooser heading. Default "Choose Your Path".

intro.description: string (optional). Introductory text below the heading.

paths.description: array (required). Array of {id, label, description, icon, accent, duration, url?} objects.

video_checkpoint.description: YouTube video with interactive quiz questions injected at specified timestamps. Video pauses at each checkpoint and the learner must answer before playback resumes. Correct/wrong feedback shown with explanation. Checkpoint state tracked per-session; completed checkpoints are skipped on replay.

title.description: string (optional). Section heading above the player.

checkpoints.description: array (required). Array of {at_seconds, question, options[], correct (0-based index), explanation} objects.

cohort_progress_board.description: Instructor-facing table showing all enrolled learners with per-module completion ticks and average quiz scores. On GAS reads live from the course progress Sheet via a2uiCohortRead. On web renders from static items[] array. Shows enrolled and active learner counts at top.

modules.description: array (optional). Array of {id, label} for column headers. Drives per-module completion dots.

items.description: array (optional, web/static). Array of {email, progress{}, updated_at} learner objects.

reflection_prompt.description: Structured free-text reflection textarea with a submit button. Saves the response to progress_store under the key "reflect:<prompt_id>". On GAS this persists to the learner's progress Sheet. Restores previously saved text on revisit.

prompt.description: string (required). The reflection question shown above the textarea.

prompt_id.description: string (optional). Key suffix for progress_store. Default "reflection".

annotation_highlight.description: Rich body text passage with clickable highlighted terms that reveal inline explanations in a panel below. No state required. Ideal for glossary enrichment, technical definitions, or legal clause annotation within learning content.

text.description: string (required). Body text passage. Annotated terms are replaced server-side.

notes.description: array (required). Array of {term, explanation, color?} objects. First match in text is highlighted.

onboarding_stepper.description: Guided step-by-step onboarding flow for first-time learners. Each step has a label, description, optional action URL (navigated to on completion), and a "Mark complete" button. Completed steps persist in progress_store and are restored on revisit. Progress count shown at top.

accent.description: string (optional). Active step indicator colour. Default

steps.description: array (required). Array of {id, icon, label, description, action_label, action_url?} objects.

case_study_card.description: Structured enterprise case study card. Sections for situation narrative, key data points (label/value/note), numbered analysis questions, and an optional model answer reveal. No state required. The model answer is hidden behind a toggle button.

data_points.description: array (optional). Array of {label, value, note?} metric blocks.

questions.description: array (optional). Array of question strings for analysis.

model_answer.description: string (optional). Model answer text shown on toggle. Hidden by default.

accent.description: string (optional). Accent colour for data point values. Default

study_timer.description: Pomodoro-style focus/break countdown timer. Configurable focus and break durations. Animated SVG progress ring. Counts completed sessions. Session-scoped only — no persistence to progress_store.

focus_mins.description: integer (optional). Focus period in minutes. Default 25.

break_mins.description: integer (optional). Break period in minutes. Default 5.

accent.description: string (optional). Ring and mode label colour. Default

rubric_card.description: Assessment rubric table. Rows are assessment criteria, columns are performance levels (e.g. Beginning/Developing/Proficient/Exemplary). Descriptor text in each cell describes that level for that criterion. Static display — no state required.

levels.description: array (optional). Performance level column headers. Default [Beginning, Developing, Proficient, Exemplary].

criteria.description: array (required). Array of {criterion, descriptors[]} objects. descriptors[] maps to levels[] by index.

spaced_repetition_card.description: Single flashcard with front/back flip animation and a post-flip confidence rating (1-5 with emoji). Writes {rating, next_days, rated_at} to progress_store under "srs:<card_id>". Next-review hint computed via simplified SM-2 (1→1d, 2→2d, 3→4d, 4→7d, 5→14d).

front.description: string (required). Front face — term or question.

back.description: string (required). Back face — answer or definition.

card_id.description: string (optional). Key for progress_store SRS data. Auto-generated if omitted.

accent.description: string (optional). Front card highlight colour. Default

leaderboard_card.description: Ranked learner leaderboard by score. On GAS reads live from the course progress Sheet via a2uiCohortRead, extracts scores for score_key, and ranks descending. On web renders from static items[]. Top 3 entries get gold/silver/bronze medal indicators. Per-row score bar visualises relative performance.

score_key.description: string (optional). Which quiz score to rank on. Default "quiz1".

items.description: array (optional, web/static). Array of {name, score} objects for static rendering.

nav_bar.description: Horizontal or vertical navigation bar linking to other named A2UI pages. Generates correct ?nav=<slug>&from=<current_slug> URLs at runtime using window._A2UI_NAV — no deployment URL needs to be hard-coded. Optionally sticky below the system nav header. Active page is highlighted automatically by comparing slugs.

label.description: string (optional). Small uppercase label above the bar.

sticky.description: boolean (optional). Whether to stick below the system nav header. Default true.

top_offset.description: integer (optional). Top offset in px when sticky. Default 52.

accent.description: string (optional). Active link accent colour. Default

links.description: array (required). Array of {nav_slug, label, icon?, active?} objects. nav_slug is the saved page slug; the correct URL is generated at runtime.

palette.description: Sets page-level CSS custom properties (accent colours, block gap) for consistent theming across all atoms. Use as the first block in any payload.

accent.description: hex colour string. Sets --a2ui-accent CSS var used by all atoms as default accent.

block_gap.description: CSS length (default 1.25rem). Vertical gap between atom blocks.

print_button.description: A button that triggers window.print() to print or save the current page as PDF.

icon.description: boolean (default true) — show printer emoji prefix

drive_image.description: Embeds a Google Drive image by file ID or share URL, converting it to the correct export URL that works inside GAS iframes.

url.description: Drive share URL OR file ID

maps_embed.description: Embeds a Google Maps location search in an iframe. Suitable for event location, office address, field ops context.

q.description: address or place name to search (required)

sheet_form.description: A form that submits data to a named Google Sheet tab via google.script.run. Requires the GAS project to be bound to a Spreadsheet.

sheet.description: sheet tab name to write to (required). Created automatically if it doesn't exist.

fields.description: array (required). Each item has label, name (optional), type (text/email/textarea/select), placeholder, required, hint, options (for select), rows (for textarea)

reveal.description: Wraps child blocks with entrance animations triggered on load. animation choices: fade_up, fade_in, slide_left, slide_right, scale_in, stagger.

animation.description: fade_up (default), fade_in, slide_left, slide_right, scale_in, stagger

delay.description: initial delay in ms before first block animates (default 0)

stagger_delay.description: ms between each block when animation is stagger (default 120)

blocks.description: array of child atoms to wrap

skeleton.description: CSS shimmer loading placeholder. skeleton_type choices: text, card, avatar_row, image, list, table.

skeleton_type.description: text (default), card, avatar_row, image, list, table

cols.description: number of columns for table type (default 3)

marquee.description: Infinite horizontal scrolling ticker. Items can be text strings, logos (image_url), or icon+label chips.

items.description: array of {text?, label?, icon?, image_url?, url?}

title.description: optional label above the marquee strip

pulse_dot.description: Pulsing status or attention indicator dot. Inline or block display.

loading_dots.description: Three-dot pulsing loader. Use during async content load or as a status indicator.

progress_ring.description: Animated SVG circular progress gauge. Animates from 0 to value on load.

show_value.description: show numeric value inside ring (default true)

confetti_burst.description: JS confetti celebration effect. trigger load fires immediately, trigger button shows a clickable button.

ripple_button.description: CTA button with a CSS ripple click effect. Optionally links to a URL.

wave_divider.description: Animated SVG wave as a decorative section separator.

floating_badge.description: An emoji or icon that bobs up and down continuously. Great for CTAs and achievement moments.

shadow.description: drop shadow that deepens on rise (default true)

shimmer_text.description: Headline text with an animated shimmer/gloss sweep across a gradient. Use for hero taglines.

number_flip.description: Slot-machine style digit reveal. Each digit flips in from above on load.

value.description: the number or string to display (required)

spotlight_card.description: A content card where a radial gradient spotlight follows the cursor. Child blocks or content field.

blocks.description: array of child atoms inside the card

animated_border.description: Card or section with a rotating conic gradient border. Child blocks or content inside.

countdown_ring.description: Circular SVG countdown that depletes as seconds tick down.

google_icon.description: A single Material Symbol icon from Google Fonts CDN. Browse 2500+ icon names at fonts.google.com/icons.

filled.description: render the filled variant (default false — outlined)

icon_badge.description: Material Symbol icon inside a coloured circular badge. Good for stat rows and feature lists.

icon_row.description: Horizontal strip of Material Symbol icon + label pairs. Good for feature/capability lists.

items.description: array of {name or icon, label or text, color?}

workspace_logo.description: A single Google Workspace product logo from Google's official CDN (fonts.gstatic.com). Falls back to coloured letter badge for unknown apps.

app.description: app name (required) — gmail, drive, docs, sheets, slides, calendar, meet, forms, chat, classroom, keep, sites, vault, groups, tasks, contacts, jamboard, admin, appsheet, currents

size.description: display size in px (default 48 — SVG scales to any size)

label.description: caption below logo (default capitalised app name; set to empty string to hide)

workspace_logo_strip.description: Horizontal strip of Google Workspace product logos. Greyscale by default, restores colour on hover.

apps.description: array of app names (default gmail, drive, docs, sheets, slides, meet)

workspace_logo_grid.description: Grid of Google Workspace product logos with labels. Good for "Integrations" or "Works with" sections.

apps.description: array of app names (default all 20 known apps)

icon_feature_grid.description: Feature grid using Material Symbol icons — better than emoji for Workspace UI contexts.

items.description: array of {icon (ligature name), title, text, color?}

icon_checklist.description: Checklist where each item has a Material Symbol icon (not just a check mark). Good for capability or requirements lists.

default_icon.description: fallback icon for items without one (default check_circle)

items.description: array of strings or {text, icon?, sublabel?, color?}

gdm_rocket_panel.description: Fixed-position canvas launch animation -- an isometric rocket climbing through exhaust trail and sparks to a HUD telemetry readout. Overlays one half of the viewport (position:fixed, non-interactive). Ported from the Google Meet Stage add-on's gdm-rocket-panel component (used in the "Apps Script is now a Workspace Core Service" playbook); graduated from stage:preview after serving as the mcp-apps page's off-catalog demo content -- the catalog's intake pipeline working as designed.

side.description: string (optional, right|left, default right -- which viewport half the overlay claims)

layer.description: string (optional, back|front, default back -- back is z-index 50, front 150, matching the original component's layer variants)

loop.description: boolean (optional, default false -- false launches once and holds at apex; true restores the original's ambient relaunch loop)

math_block.description: Native MathML equation typeset with the CSS `math` generic font family (STIX / Latin Modern Math) — no JavaScript, no external library. The agent emits standard MathML; the surface draws it. Great for maths and science content — fractions, roots, integrals, matrices — rendered with correct spacing and alignment.

mathml.description: string. MathML markup — inner content or a full <math> element. Active content (script, annotation-xml, on* handlers, javascript:) is stripped before the markup is rendered.

caption.description: string (optional). Caption shown below the equation.
